summary: Coulomb's law gives the electric force between two point charges: F = k·q₁·q₂/r². Like gravity it is inverse-square, so the force falls off sharply with distance.
method: Identify the two charges q₁ and q₂ and the distance r between them.
Decide which quantity is unknown — force, a charge, or the distance.
Rearrange F = k·q₁·q₂/r²; solving for r involves a square root.
Use SI units (coulombs, metres); the calculator supplies k ≈ 8.99 × 10⁹ N·m²/C².
Interpret the sign: like charges repel, unlike charges attract.

example.title: Force between two small charges
steps: The unknown is F, so use F = k·q₁·q₂/r².
